mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, it signals a confirmed labor market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized real sector expansion, which reduces supply chain friction and supports near-term earnings growth, yielding positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5 percent over any rolling 3-year window, indicating the interaction lacks predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to raise policy rates, which compresses equity risk premiums and discount rates, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of tightening episodes, or the cross-sectional correlation between rate hikes and index declines drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales directly reflect increasing consumer demand, which translates to higher near-term corporate revenues and positive short-term equity momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, indicating retail sales momentum does not translate to short-term equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates by more than 1.0 percent month-over-month, it confirms supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input bottlenecks and supports profit margins, leading to positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns average less than 0.5 percent over the 20-day horizon following the threshold crossing, or the hit rate falls below 55 percent in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment validate a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity performance by reducing wage-price spiral fears.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with a p-value greater than 0.10, or forward returns are indistinguishable from the market benchmark over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, real GDP growth outpacing inflation signals healthy demand recovery without overheating, which historically supports positive equity returns over the following month by validating corporate earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of identified recovery episodes, or the predictive correlation between real GDP growth and index returns drops below 0.05.
